name: midday
description: time_ratio = 0.5 (halfway through the trading day by wall-clock). LOAD-BEARING DIVERGENCE FIXTURE: this is the single largest behavioral-change point between the pre-M3 log10(1+9t) heuristic and the post-M3 1-sqrt(1-t) derivation. At t=0.5, the pre-M3 curve had decay=0.7404 and dynamic_multiplier=0.7596 (already aggressively tight midday); the post-M3 curve has decay=0.2929 and dynamic_multiplier=1.2071 -- about 0.45 percentage-points-of-stop WIDER midday. A wrong impl that left the log10 heuristic in place would diverge by ~0.45 here. This is the M3 behavioral budget concentrated in a single number.
provenance: DERIVED from the closed-form THEORY formula f(t) = 1 - sqrt(1 - t) (Danielsson & Zigrand, 2003, LSE FMG DP-439). NOT captured from compute_time_squeeze_decay output -- D-2 load-bearing per docs/research/m3-provenance/literature-pass.md §3.1.
